title: A study guide for the $l^2$ Decoupling Theorem

thm:1.1.summary: Sharp (up to $\delta^{-\epsilon}$) bound on the decoupling constant for the truncated paraboloid: $\mathrm{Dec}_n(\delta,p)\lesssim_{\epsilon,p,n}\delta^{-\epsilon}$ for $2\le p\le\tfrac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$. A streamlined, self-contained re-proof of the Bourgain–Demeter $l^2$ decoupling theorem with cleaner notation and bookkeeping.

lem:4.1.summary: Abstract weight-transfer lemma: if $O_1,O_2$ on weights satisfy sub/super-linearity and $O_1(1_B)\lesssim O_2(w_{B,E})$ on $R$-cubes, then $O_1(w_B)\lesssim O_2(w_B)$. Lets sharp-cutoff estimates be upgraded to weighted ones, with explicit tail-decay parameter $E$.

cor:4.3.summary: Reverse Hölder / local constancy for the extension operator: for $q\ge p\ge1$, an $R^{-1}$-cube $Q$ and an $R$-cube $B$, $\|E_Qg\|_{L^q_\sharp(w_{B,E})}\lesssim\|E_Qg\|_{L^p_\sharp(w_{B,Ep/q})}$. $E_Qg$ is essentially constant on $R$-balls.

thm:5.1.summary: Equivalent formulation: decoupling $\mathrm{Dec}_n(\delta,p)\lesssim\delta^{-\epsilon}$ is equivalent to a localized inequality for $f$ Fourier-supported in the $1/R$-neighborhood of the paraboloid over $[0,1]^{n-1}$, with explicit weight $w_{B_R,F}$ and parameter management. The clean statement the rest of the argument runs on.

prop:6.1.summary: $L^2$ decoupling (the trivial flat case): for a cube $Q$ with $l(Q)\ge R^{-1}$, $\|E_Qg\|_{L^2(w_{B_R})}\lesssim(\sum_{q\in\mathrm{Part}_{1/R}(Q)}\|E_qg\|_{L^2(w_{B_R})}^2)^{1/2}$. Plancherel — the $p=2$ endpoint, no curvature needed.

prop:7.1.summary: Parabolic rescaling: for a $\sigma^{1/2}$-cube $Q\subset[0,1]^{n-1}$ and $B$ of radius $\ge\delta^{-1}$, $\|E_Qg\|_{L^p(w_B)}\lesssim\mathrm{Dec}_p(\delta/\sigma,\dots)(\sum_{q\subset Q,\,l(q)=\delta^{1/2}}\|E_qg\|^2)^{1/2}$. The affine-invariance step that enables the multiscale self-improvement.

def:8.1.summary: Cubes $Q_1,\dots,Q_n\subset[0,1]^{n-1}$ are $\nu$-transverse if the parallelepiped spanned by the paraboloid's unit normals over the $Q_i$ has volume $>\nu$. The transversality hypothesis for the multilinear estimates.

thm:8.2.summary:
Linear-versus-multilinear decoupling: assuming the theorem in dimension $n-1$, the linear decoupling constant is controlled by $C_
u\delta^{-\epsilon(
u)}$ times the $
u$-transverse multilinear constant, with $\epsilon(
u)	o0$. The Bourgain–Guth broad/narrow reduction, cleanly stated, with induction on dimension.

prop:8.4.summary: Broad/narrow inequality ($n=3$): assuming $\mathrm{Dec}_2(\delta,p)\lesssim\delta^{-\epsilon}$, for each $m\ge1$, $R\ge K^{2^m}$, $\|Eg\|_{L^p(w_{B_R,E})}\le C_\epsilon K^\epsilon[(\sum_\alpha\dots)+\dots]$ — the dichotomy with explicit scale parameter $m$.

prop:8.5.summary: Cap-localized broad/narrow ($n=3$): the same split applied inside a square $\tau$ of side $\ge R^{-1/2}K^{2^{m-1}}$, the rescaled building block of the iteration.

thm:9.1.summary: Multilinear Kakeya for tiles: for $n$ families of $R^{1/2}\times\cdots\times R^{1/2}\times R$ tiles with the long axes pointing in $\nu$-transverse directions, the appropriate $L^{n/(n-1)}$ overlap integral is controlled. The geometric input (a packaging of Guth's multilinear Kakeya).

thm:9.2.summary: From multilinear Kakeya to multilinear decoupling (ball inflation): for $\nu$-transverse cubes $Q_1,\dots,Q_n$, $p\ge\tfrac{2n}{n-1}$, and $B$ of radius $\delta^{-2}$ partitioned into $\delta^{-1}$-balls $\Delta$, the averaged product of $l^2$-square-functions over the $\Delta$ is bounded by the same over $B$, up to $\delta^{-\epsilon}$. The engine that gains at each scale.

prop:10.1.summary: Single ball-inflation cycle: $A_p(1,B^2,1,g)\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu}\delta^{-\epsilon}A_p(2,B^2,2,g)^{1-\kappa_p}D_p(1,B^2,g)^{\kappa_p}$. One step of the iteration, with exponent $\kappa_p$ fixed by interpolation.

prop:10.2.summary: Single cycle on a $2^M$-cube, uniformly in $M$: $A_p(1,B^M,1,g)\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu}\delta^{-\epsilon}A_p(2,B^M,2,g)^{1-\kappa_p}D_p(1,B^M,g)^{\kappa_p}$. The cycle that survives passing to many nested scales.

prop:10.3.summary: Cycle at level $2^l$: for $l+1\le m$, $A_p(2^l,B^{2^m},2^l,g)\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu}\delta^{-2^l\epsilon}A_p(2^{l+1},\dots)^{1-\kappa_p}D_p(2^l,\dots)^{\kappa_p}$. The recursion at each level of the binary scale ladder.

prop:10.4.summary: $m$-fold unrolled iterate: $A_p(1,B^{2^m},1,g)\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu,m}\delta^{-\epsilon}A_p(2^{m-1},\dots)^{(1-\kappa_p)^{m-1}}\prod_{l=0}^{m-2}D_p(2^l,\dots)^{\kappa_p(1-\kappa_p)^l}$. Composing the cycles down the scale ladder.

thm:11.1.summary: The final argument: $A_p(1,B^{2^m},1,g)\lesssim_{\epsilon,\nu,m}\delta^{-(\eta_p+\epsilon)(2^m-\frac{2\kappa_p}{\cdots})}\cdots$ — sending $m\to\infty$ and optimizing forces $\eta_p=0$, i.e. $\mathrm{Dec}_n(\delta,p)\lesssim\delta^{-\epsilon}$, which is thm:1.1.

edges.note: The sharp decoupling bound is the $m\to\infty$ limit of the final iterated inequality.
Works with the equivalent localized formulation throughout.
The final inequality is the $m$-fold unrolled iterate with the decoupling factors estimated by the inductive hypothesis.
Composes the level-$2^l$ cycles down the binary scale ladder.
The level-$2^l$ recursion is the rescaled form of the single cycle on a $2^M$-cube.
Uniform-in-$M$ version of the basic ball-inflation cycle.
Each cycle is an application of the multilinear decoupling (ball-inflation) inequality.
...combined with the linear-vs-multilinear reduction to put the narrow term back into linear form.
Multilinear decoupling is bootstrapped from the multilinear Kakeya inequality for tiles.
The $L^2$ (flat) decoupling handles the orthogonality at the finest scale.
Local constancy normalizes the $L^p$ norms in the ball-inflation argument.
The reduction runs through the quantitative broad/narrow inequality.
Sub-cubes are rescaled to unit scale between steps.
Transversality is the regime where the multilinear term is gainful.
The weight-transfer lemma converts the sharp-cutoff inequality to the weighted equivalent form.
The narrow term is recognized as a multilinear-decoupling situation.

main_result.description: The sharp $l^2$ decoupling theorem for the paraboloid, re-proved.

setup_and_tools.description: Weight transfer, local constancy, the equivalent localized formulation, the trivial $L^2$ case, and parabolic rescaling.

linear_vs_multilinear.description: The Bourgain–Guth broad/narrow dichotomy and its quantitative forms, with induction on dimension.

multilinear_core.description: Multilinear Kakeya for tiles, the ball-inflation inequality it yields, and the iteration scheme (single cycle, uniform-in-$M$, level-$2^l$, $m$-fold) closed by the final argument.

BD-l2.title: The proof of the l^2 Decoupling Conjecture

Guth:MultilinearKakeya.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality

BD-curves.title: Decouplings for curves and hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature
